ace: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Acehnese.
acre: noun. An English unit of land area (symbol: a. or ac.) originally denoting a day 's ploughing for a yoke of oxen, now standardized as 4,840
ale: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Aleut.
alec: noun. An anchovy or herring, especially pickled or dried.
all: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Allar.
allure: noun. The power to attract, entice; the quality causing attraction.
are: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Western Arrernte.
car: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Kari'na.
carl: noun. A rude, rustic man; a churl.
caul: noun. A style of close-fitting circular cap worn by women in the sixteenth century and later, often made of linen.
clue: noun. A strand of yarn etc. as used to guide one through a labyrinth; something which points the way, a guide.
cruel: adjective. Intentionally causing or reveling in pain and suffering; merciless, heartless.
cue: noun. The name of the Latin script letter Q / q.
cull: verb. To pick or take someone or something (from a larger group).
culler: noun. One who picks or chooses.
cur: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Chhulung.
ear: noun. The organ of hearing, consisting of the pinna or auricle, auditory canal, eardrum, malleus, incus, stapes and cochlea.
earl: noun. A British or Irish nobleman next in rank above a viscount and below a marquess; equivalent to a European count.
ecu: noun. The European Currency Unit (symbol ₠), a currency used in the European Community before the euro.
ell: symbol. ISO 639-2 /T & ISO 639-3 language code for Greek.
era: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Eravallan.
lac: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Lacandon.
lace: noun. A light fabric containing patterns of holes, usually built up from a single thread.
lacer: noun. A person or thing that laces.
lar: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Larteh.
laurel: noun. An evergreen shrub of species Laurus nobilis, having aromatic leaves of a lanceolate shape, with clusters of small, yellowish white
lea: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Lega-Shabunda.
leu: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Kara (New Guinea).
luce: noun. The pike, Esox lucius, when fully grown.
lucre: noun. Money, riches, or wealth, especially when seen as having a corrupting effect or causing greed, or obtained in an underhanded manner.
race: noun. A contest between people, animals, vehicles, etc. where the goal is to be the first to reach some objective.
rale: noun. An abnormal clicking, rattling or crackling sound, made by one or both lungs and heard with a stethoscope, caused by the popping open
real: adjective. True, genuine, not merely nominal or apparent.
recall: verb. To withdraw, retract (one's words etc.); to revoke (an order).
rue: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Carpathian Rusyn.
ulcer: noun. An open sore of the skin, eyes or mucous membrane, often caused by an initial abrasion and generally maintained by an inflammation
urea: noun. A water-soluble organic compound, CO(NH 2) 2, formed by the metabolism of proteins and excreted in the urine.
ureal: adjective. Of or pertaining to urea.
